title: Watcher persistence across restarts
description:
What: On nie-cli startup (inside chat() before spawning watcher), load all PaymentSessions in Watching state from the wallet DB and register their addresses in AddressWatchRegistry.

Algorithm:
1. Call wallet_store.all_sessions() (returns Vec<PaymentSession>).
2. Filter: retain only sessions where state == PaymentState::Watching and address.is_some().
3. For each such session: registry.register(session.address.unwrap(), session.id).
4. Log the count at debug level: 'Restored N watching sessions into watcher registry'.

Defensive programming:
- Silently skip sessions missing address (should not happen, but guard defensively).
- This runs at startup before the watcher task is spawned; no concurrency concern here.
- Do not log any session payment amounts or address alongside key material.

Tests:
- test_persistence_reload: create wallet DB with 2 Watching sessions and 1 Complete session, call the reload logic, assert registry contains exactly 2 entries.
- test_persistence_reload_no_address: session without address is skipped, no panic.

Acceptance: cargo test passes; Complete sessions not loaded into registry; clippy clean.
close_reason: watch_registry startup restore added to chat(); sessions_to_watch() loads Watching sessions into registry; 268 tests pass; clippy clean
